text: fat is 48.8g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
carbs is 8.7g. fibre is where things start to fall off. for a seed, that is a huge win.
protein is 31.6g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 5% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 175% DV, zinc at 66% DV, iron at 44% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

the upsides first. tiny food with serious nutrient density because seeds often overperform for their size. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters. usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths.

the drawbacks next. calorie density still stacks up fast because tiny foods can still overshoot fast. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.
A tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
